Here's how you know

- Official websites use .mass.gov



A .mass.gov website belongs to an official government organization in Massachusetts.

- Secure websites use HTTPS certificate



A lock icon
(

)
or https:// means you've safely connected to the official website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites.


# Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination   (MCAD)

## Notices & Alerts  HideExpand

- ### notice Notice of Public Meeting – May 12, 2026Updated Apr. 28, 2026, 01:54 pm+





[Notice of MCAD Public Meeting May 12, 2026 at 10:00 AM via YouTube](https://mass.gov/event/mcad-public-meeting-may-12-2026-05-12-2026 "Learn more Notice of Public Meeting – May 12, 2026")

- ### notice Coming Soon: New MCAD Case Management System & Online Complaint FilingUpdated Mar. 2, 2026, 09:00 am+





[The Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination (MCAD) is pleased to announce the upcoming launch of a new online portal for discrimination complaint filing.](https://www.mass.gov/alerts/coming-soon-new-mcad-case-management-system-online-complaint-filing#5220456 "Learn more Coming Soon: New MCAD Case Management System & Online Complaint Filing")


The Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination (MCAD) is the independent state agency that enforces the Massachusetts anti-discrimination laws by investigating complaints of discrimination in employment, housing, public accommodations, and other aspects of everyday life. Pursuant to its mission to eradicate discrimination in the Commonwealth, the MCAD also offers remedial and preventative training, and publishes resources online such as model policies, posters, and guides.

## Navigating Discrimination in Massachusetts

![Navigating Discrimination in Massachusetts](https://www.mass.gov/files/styles/565x/public/2025-06/MOLA-MCAD%20Title%20Page.jpg?itok=zCnC8tDy)

Developed in partnership with the Mayor’s Office of LGBTQIA2S+ Advancement (MOLA), the Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination (MCAD), and Northeastern University, this guidebook compiles essential information on where and how to report discriminatory treatment across the 15 agencies in Massachusetts that handle complaints of discrimination and hate crimes. "Connecting you with the right agency, the right process, and your right to act."

[Discrimination Guidebook](https://www.boston.gov/sites/default/files/file/2025/05/Final%20Completed%20Guidebook%20.pdf)
